raw_consumer_statement:
On or about XX/XX/year>, my credit card was used for a {$300.00} transaction at XXXX without my authorization. Shortly after the attempted transaction, I received a text message from the merchant asking me to confirm whether I recognized the purchase. I responded NO multiple times, clearly denying authorization. 

Despite receiving my denial, the merchant proceeded to process the charge. I immediately disputed the transaction with American Express and provided documentation showing that I did not authorize the purchase and that the merchant was notified before completing the transaction. 

American Express initially issued a provisional credit, indicating acknowledgment of a valid billing dispute. However, despite the evidence provided, American Express later rebilled my account without conducting a reasonable investigation or addressing the documentation I submitted.

This action violates the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) and Regulation Z ( 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 ), which require creditors to conduct a reasonable investigation before rebilling a disputed charge. Reinstating the charge without properly evaluating the evidence is improper and unlawful. 

I have acted promptly, in good faith, and fully cooperated with the investigation. The merchants own verification message and my denial of authorization clearly demonstrate that this transaction was unauthorized and should never have been completed.
